chunks.title: Introduction to Sardine Fasting
chunks.summary: The video begins with a discussion on the trend of sardine fasting, claiming benefits such as weight loss and ketosis. The speaker expresses skepticism about these claims and hints at potential issues associated with sardine consumption.
chunks.transcript: Sardine fasting. It's a huge trend right now. People are saying you can lose weight, go into ketosis easily, reset your metabolism, detox bollocks,  Sardine fasting is a horrible idea. I'm going to break it all down for you in this video. I'm going to tell you about heavy metals that are in your sardines in significant amounts that you may not know about that could be an issue. I'm also going to tell you about endocrine disruptors in your sardines. Yes, hormone disrupting compounds in your sardines that are messing with your good hormones. And at the end of this video, I will tell you the best way to eat sardines if you insist on eating these gross little dudes. I don't think they're worth eating at all. But if you absolutely want to eat sardines and you want them in your diet, I will tell you the best way to do that at the end of this video. And I hope this will improve your health. So, where did this whole

chunks.title: Potential Health Risks of Sardines
chunks.summary: The speaker highlights significant concerns regarding heavy metals and endocrine disruptors found in sardines. They discuss BPA, a harmful chemical often present in canned sardines, and its potential health impacts.
chunks.transcript: trend of sardine fasting come from? It's been popularized by other people on YouTube who claim that it can reset your metabolism. I think these claims are pretty far-fetched. There's really no evidence that sardine fasting does anything good for humans. There's no studies on it at all. And as I said previously, there are some pretty big downsides. The thing that's good about sardine fasting is you're going to lose weight because sardines are so gross that nobody wants to eat much of them. And they will put you into ketosis because there's absolutely no carbs in sardines. Whether or not you think ketosis is a good thing is up for debate. I personally believe that ketosis is not a panacea for humans. And so the idea that fasting on sardines will put you into ketosis doesn't really hold much weight for me. What really does hold weight for me is the analysis of the potential toxins in the sardines. So, if you're doing a sardine fast, you're eating only sardines for 72 hours for 3 days. Some people are even doing seven or 10 day sardine fasts, which of course you're going to lose weight, right? These are gross. But what's the big issue here? chunks.title: Omega-3 Fatty Acids in Sardines
chunks.summary: This segment covers the omega-3 fatty acids present in sardines, emphasizing their fragility and potential rancidity in canned fish. The speaker argues that omega-3s can be sourced more safely from other foods like grass-fed meat.
chunks.transcript: smaller. It's a nightmare. You do not want to be consuming plastic or plasticizers or chemical like BPA if you can avoid them. And when you're eating sardines, especially if you're doing a sardine fast, you are packing your body full of BPA. There's also a trend among women saying that eating a bunch of sardines can improve your skin. It makes sense cuz sardines are full fish. They have bones. They have ligaments. They have skin. They're full of collagen. I think a lot of women and even men don't get enough collagen. But with this amount of BPA contamination in sardines, especially disrupting women's hormones, there are much better ways to get collagen to improve the quality of your skin. The easiest ways to get enough collagen in your diet are to make something like bone broth from grass-fed beef bones or tendonous ligaments parts of the animal or use a good quality third party tested collagen supplement. We make an amazing one from Lineage. I'll put it there. But that's not it. There's more. There are more issues with sardines. Another potential problem with sardines is, well, they're fish, which means they have high levels of omega-3s. Now, omega-3s, like EPA and DHA, are generally healthy for humans. You can get them from egg yolks. You can get them from grass-fed meat, but for some reason, people seem obsessed with getting them from fish. This isn't a problem if you're eating a fish fresh. In here, fish still have those compounds, but they're sitting in the oil, heat packed on a shelf for years. So, this is an important point with your sardines to consider. chunks.title: Arsenic Levels in Sardines
chunks.summary: The speaker explains the presence of organic arsenic in sardines and compares it to other sources like rice. They caution about the potential rise in arsenic levels through sardine consumption.
chunks.transcript: sardines is arsenic. Some people will say sardines are low in mercury. This is true. Other fish like tuna, especially bigger fish, swordfish, tons of mercury. That's a big problem for humans. But sardines, not a lot of mercury. They're very high in arsenic though. There's actually a study from Brazil that tested 120 samples of sardines and declared all of them unfit for chronic consumption due to exceeding the limits of arsenic. But there's an asterisk here. The arsenic in sardines is organic arsenic. It's arsenobetane, which the human body can filter out more easily than inorganic arsenic. That's the type of arsenic found in water or rice. Now, a word about water and rice. If you're eating rice, you're getting a lot of arsenic. You should test your arsenic levels. That's the bad type of arsenic. Brown rice is worse than white rice. And of the white rice, jasmine rice and basmati rice have lower amounts of arsenic. If you want to decrease the amount of arsenic in your rice, this should probably be a whole separate video, but you do something called the spaghetti method where you add the rice to a bunch of water and then you pour the water off. That's a separate video. We're talking about sardines right now, but I wanted to give you guys the value on the rice. Know that if you're eating rice, you're getting far more arsenic than you are from sardines effectively. But sardines do contain a significant amount of arsenic. It's arsenobetane. Your body can get rid of it, but it can still raise your arsenic levels significantly. chunks.title: Recommendations for Consuming Sardines
chunks.summary: In the final segment, the speaker offers guidance for those who still wish to include sardines in their diet. They suggest opting for sardines packed in salt water and checking expiration dates to minimize health risks.
chunks.transcript: Arsenic is a known carcinogen heavy metal. We don't need extra amounts of heavy metals in our diet, guys. These are things like lead, mercury, cadmium, and arsenic. And they're high in sardines. So now finally, as I promised you, if you choose to eat sardines, let me give you the recommendations on how to do this best. Let me first say that sardines are far better than junk food. Sardines are not the end of the world. They're not the worst food in the world. They're not a superfood and they're not in my kitchen. These are going in the trash immediately after I finish this video, especially because most of them are packed with seed oils anyway. But if you are absolutely insistent on including sardines in your diet, here's how I would do it. I would choose sardines packed in salt water, not in any sort of oil. Salt water is going to leech less of the BPA from the lining of the sardines can into the sardines. And if you can find sardines packed in glass, that's going to be even better because there's no BPA in glass. The second thing to consider here is look at the expiration date on your sardines. This one has an expiration date of December 12th, 2030. This means this was probably packed 5 years from this date, December 12th, 2025. Meaning these sardines are probably only 2 months old in this can. Some of these other cans, in contrast, have expiration dates of September 2028. I don't know if they were packed in 2023 or 2024, but they're definitely older sardines that have been sitting in the can longer. That means they're going to have more BPA in the oil, and those fragile omega-3 fats are going to be more likely to be oxidized and rancid. No bueno. So, in summary, if you want to eat sardines, check the expiration date, get them in glass, get them packed in salt water, and check your arsenic levels. And let me just say it, guys. I would far rather do a 72-hour fast of grass-fed beef, grass-fed ground beef, and steak, and sardines. This is a trend that in my mind needs to die. It should never have become popular in the first place. I think fish are simply too polluted today to make a big part of our diet. And people will inevitably say, "What is left to eat?" Knowledge is power. Know better, do better. On my website, paulsaladinomd.com, there is an eating guide. I lay it all out for you. I'll tell you what I think the most toxic and least toxic foods are. And sardines are not on the list. I'm sorry, but there are many other good foods for humans to eat. So, don't get this victim mindset that says there's nothing else left to eat. That's silly. Don't fall into that trap. There's plenty of good foods to eat, and I've shown you guys what I think are the best based on my research time and time again.
